claim_text: simonyan2014 introduces the VGG architecture, used as the deep feature extractor in many DFD metrics (LPIPS uses VGG).

sub_claims.text: Simonyan & Zisserman 2014 introduces the VGG convolutional network architecture (very deep ConvNets with 16-19 weight layers using 3x3 convolution filters, branded as 'VGG' Net-D / Net-E by the authors).

evidence.snippet: In this work we investigate the effect of the convolutional network depth on its accuracy in the large-scale image recognition setting. Our main contribution is a thorough evaluation of networks of increasing depth using an architecture with very small (3 × 3) convolution filters, which shows that a significant improvement on the prior-art configurations can be achieved by pushing the depth to 16-19 weight layers.

The ConvNet configurations, evaluated in this paper, are outlined in Table 1, one per column. In the following we will refer to the nets by their names (A-E). All configurations follow the generic design presented in Sect. 2.1, and differ only in the depth: from 11 weight layers in the network A (8 conv. and 3 FC layers) to 19 weight layers in the network E (16 conv. and 3 FC layers).

During training, the input to our ConvNets is a fixed-size 224 × 224 RGB image... The image is passed through a stack of convolutional (conv.) layers, where we use filters with a very small receptive field: 3 × 3 (which is the smallest size to capture the notion of left/right, up/down, center).

For the majority of experiments, we used the validation set as the test set. Certain experiments were also carried out on the test set and submitted to the official ILSVRC server as a "VGG" team entry to the ILSVRC-2014 competition (Russakovsky et al., 2014).

evidence.section: COMPARISON WITH THE STATE OF THE ART.txt
evidence.snippet: In the classification task of ILSVRC-2014 challenge (Russakovsky et al., 2014), our "VGG" team secured the 2nd place with 7.3% test error using an ensemble of 7 models. After the submission, we decreased the error rate to 6.8% using an ensemble of 2 models.
Method VOC-2012 (mean AP) ... VGG Net-D & Net-E, image-only 79.2 VGG Net-D & Net-E, image and bounding box 84.0

sub_claims.nuance: Source directly and verbatim introduces the architecture and self-brands it as 'VGG' (team name and Net-D / Net-E result rows). The 16-layer Net-D and 19-layer Net-E configurations are the canonical VGG-16 and VGG-19. The downstream half of the manuscript sentence ('used as the deep feature extractor in many DFD metrics; LPIPS uses VGG') is out of scope for this source (simonyan2014 predates LPIPS) and must be grounded against zhang2018 / the LPIPS paper rather than against simonyan2014; it does not contradict this source.

remediation.suggested_edit: Keep simonyan2014 as the citation for 'introduces the VGG architecture', and add a separate citation to zhang2018 (LPIPS) for the downstream claim that 'LPIPS uses VGG as a feature extractor'. Recommended phrasing: 'simonyan2014 introduces the VGG architecture, which is used as the deep feature extractor in many DFD metrics (e.g., LPIPS [zhang2018]).' This splits cite responsibility cleanly: simonyan2014 owns the architecture introduction; zhang2018 owns the LPIPS-uses-VGG claim.

phrasings_tried: VGG / VGG team / VGG Net-D / VGG Net-E
16-19 weight layers / 16 weight layers / 19 weight layers
3 x 3 convolution filters / very small receptive field

section_checklist.section: COMPARISON WITH THE STATE OF THE ART.txt

attestation.indirect_attribution_check: Direct attribution. Simonyan & Zisserman 2014 is the primary VGG paper; the architecture (VGG-16 = Net-D, VGG-19 = Net-E) is introduced and named by the authors themselves in this work. No indirect chain.
attestation.out_of_context_check: The manuscript's first half (introducing VGG) matches the source's own framing exactly. The second half (LPIPS / DFD usage of VGG as feature extractor) is downstream usage that this source does not and cannot speak to — simonyan2014 predates LPIPS and contains no mention of perceptual-distance use. That portion needs verification against the LPIPS source (zhang2018), not against simonyan2014, but it does not contradict simonyan2014; it is simply outside its scope.
